posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Dear experts,
I would like to have a plot with nuisance parameters as they got after the fit.
In my old notes which do not work with the present version I have commands:
combine -M MaxLikelihoodFit combined.txt --plots --saveShapes --saveWithUncertainties --saveNormalizations
it produced mlfit.root. Get nuisance parameters with command:
python diffNuisances.py mlfit.root -g outputfile.root
In outputfile.root there are plots of the fitted values of the nuisance parameters and their post-fit uncertainties as well as a plot showing directly a comparison of the post-fit to pre-fit nuisance uncertainties.
I can’t find in the present description of combine how it all looks like now.
Can you help, please.
Thanks, Sasha.
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts,

I would like to have a plot with nuisance parameters as they got after the fit.
In my old notes which do not work with the present version I have commands:

combine -M MaxLikelihoodFit combined.txt --plots --saveShapes --saveWithUncertainties --saveNormalizations

it produced mlfit.root. Get nuisance parameters with command:

python diffNuisances.py mlfit.root -g outputfile.root

In outputfile.root there are plots of the fitted values of the nuisance parameters and their post-fit uncertainties as well as a plot showing directly a comparison of the post-fit to pre-fit nuisance uncertainties.

I can’t find in the present description of combine how it all looks like now.
Can you help, please.

Thanks, Sasha.

posts.name: Adinda de Wit
posts.text:
Hi Sasha,
Maybe have a look at the documentation: https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/latest/part3/nonstandard/#pre-and-post-fit-nuisance-parameters ?
Instead of doing the comparison from the FitDiagnostics result with diffNuisances, you could also consider producing the impacts, described here, which probably give you the information you want, but also more: https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/latest/part3/nonstandard/#nuisance-parameter-impacts
Adinda
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Sasha,

Maybe have a look at the documentation: https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/latest/part3/nonstandard/#pre-and-post-fit-nuisance-parameters ?

Instead of doing the comparison from the FitDiagnostics result with diffNuisances, you could also consider producing the impacts, described here, which probably give you the information you want, but also more: https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/latest/part3/nonstandard/#nuisance-parameter-impacts

Adinda

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Hi Adinda,
Thanks !
I used first
combine -M FitDiagnostics -d combined.txt --robustFit=1 --rMin 0 --rMax 120 --redefineSignalPOIs width --setParameters mean=27.2 --freezeParameters mean,r
which gives me what I wanted
— FitDiagnostics —
Best fit width: 3.24332  -1.64329/+4.78415  (68% CL)
and then I use
python diffNuisances.py higgsCombineTest.FitDiagnostics.mH120.root --abs --all -g outputfile.root
which gives me an error:
Singularity> python diffNuisances.py higgsCombineTest.FitDiagnostics.mH120.root --abs --all -g outputfile.root
Traceback (most recent call last):
File “diffNuisances.py”, line 181, in
raise RuntimeError(“File %s does not contain the output of the signal fit ‘fit_s’” % args[0])
RuntimeError: File higgsCombineTest.FitDiagnostics.mH120.root does not contain the output of the signal fit ‘fit_s’
Singularity>
Cheers, Sasha.

posts.name: Giacomo Ortona
posts.text:
Dear Sasha,
As written in the documentation sent by Adinda, the diffNuisances script runs on a different file. By default, it should be called fitDiagnostics.root, you can check which files were produced by combine to be sure about the filename.

It is possible to compare pre-fit and post-fit nuisance parameter values with the script diffNuisances.py. Taking as input a fitDiagnostics.root

Cheers,
Giacomo
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Sasha,

As written in the documentation sent by Adinda, the diffNuisances script runs on a different file. By default, it should be called fitDiagnostics.root, you can check which files were produced by combine to be sure about the filename.

It is possible to compare pre-fit and post-fit nuisance parameter values with the script diffNuisances.py. Taking as input a fitDiagnostics.root

Cheers,
Giacomo

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Hi Giacomo,
ah, yes , my command
combine -M FitDiagnostics -d combined.txt --robustFit=1 --rMin 0 --rMax 120 --redefineSignalPOIs width --setParameters mean=27.2 --freezeParameters mean,r
produces me also this file
fitDiagnosticsTest.root
but when I run on it
python diffNuisances.py fitDiagnosticsTest.root --abs --all -g outputfile.root
it gives me now error:

python diffNuisances.py fitDiagnosticsTest.root --abs --all -g outputfile.root
Traceback (most recent call last):
File “diffNuisances.py”, line 235, in
error_poi = fpf_s.find(options.poi).getError()
AttributeError: ‘RooAbsArg’ object has no attribute ‘getError’

Thanks, Sasha.
posts.cooked_html:

python diffNuisances.py fitDiagnosticsTest.root --abs --all -g outputfile.root
Traceback (most recent call last):
File “diffNuisances.py”, line 235, in
error_poi = fpf_s.find(options.poi).getError()
AttributeError: ‘RooAbsArg’ object has no attribute ‘getError’

Thanks, Sasha.

posts.name: Giacomo Ortona
posts.text:
Hi Sasha,
Can you try adding the option -p width to the diffNuisances command? Otherwise, the script will assume the POI is called r.
Cheers,
Giacomo
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Sasha,

Can you try adding the option -p width to the diffNuisances command? Otherwise, the script will assume the POI is called r.

Cheers,
Giacomo

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
ah, it works !  Thank you very much Giacomo.
Cheers, Sasha.
posts.cooked_html:
ah, it works ! Thank you very much Giacomo.

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Hi Giacomo,
I got a strange plot (attached) of nuisance parameters  which I have in my datacards as:
Rsr1_2018_2017  param  1.53 0.04
Rsr2_2018_2017  param  1.52 0.11
Rsr3_2018_2017  param  1.50 0.04
The minus errors of pre-fit on the plot go to zero …
I attached also printout of command:
python diffNuisances.py -p width fitDiagnosticsTest.root --abs --all -g outputfile.root
where minus errors and the same as plus ones,
nuisance.pdf (14.5 KB)
out.txt (6.0 KB)
Is it a problem with plotting or my mistake ?
Thanks, Sasha.
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Giacomo,

Rsr1_2018_2017 param 1.53 0.04

Rsr3_2018_2017 param 1.50 0.04

nuisance.pdf (14.5 KB)

Thanks, Sasha.

posts.name: Giacomo Ortona
posts.text:
Dear Sasha,
This is just a plotting issue. I don’t remember the exact reason for it, but it happens on the parameters declared as “param” with a mean value different from 0 in the datacard. I don’t think you need to worry about it.
Best regards,
Giacomo
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Sasha,

Best regards,
Giacomo
